like.e: I like to play with my friends.

life.d: the time from birth until death
life.e: My dog has been my best friend for my whole life.

last.d: the final one in a group or series
last.e: I was the last one to finish my homework.

long.d: having a great distance from one end to the other
long.e: My dog has a long tail that wags back and forth.

love.d: A strong feeling of caring for someone or something
love.e: I love playing with my dog.

look.d: To use your eyes to see something
look.e: I look at the birds in the sky.

little.e: I have a little dog that is very cute.

left.d: The side that is opposite of right
left.e: I left my backpack at school.

let.e: Mom let me stay up late on my birthday.

lot.e: I have a lot of fun playing with my toys.

live.e: The animals live in the zoo.

later.d: At a time after the present
later.e: I'll play with you later, after I finish my homework.

looking.d: To use your eyes to see or search for something
looking.e: The child was looking for her lost toy.

law.d: A rule that everyone has to follow
law.e: The law says we have to stop at red lights.

less.d: Not as much or not as many
less.e: I have less toys than my sister.

line.d: A row of things or people
line.e: There was a long line at the store.

least.e: She has the least amount of toys in the class.

level.d: The position or rank of something.
level.e: This is the highest level of the game.

local.d: Relating to a small area or community.
local.e: The local park is a nice place to play.

large.e: The large dog jumped over the fence to play.

lost.d: When you can't find something you had
lost.e: I lost my favorite toy and looked everywhere for it.

late.d: happening or arriving after the expected or usual time
late.e: I was late for school because I woke up late.

leave.d: to go away from a place
leave.e: I need to leave for school soon.

light.d: the bright thing that helps us see
light.e: The sun gives us light during the day.

low.d: Not high, close to the ground
low.e: The bird flew low over the field.

living.e: The family's pet cat is still living and healthy.

land.d: The ground or area of earth
land.e: I like to play on the grassy land in the park.

looks.d: The way something or someone appears
looks.e: The park looks beautiful in the sunshine.

list.d: A group of items written down one after another
list.e: I made a list of all the things I need to do.

la.d: A musical note that is part of the scale
la.e: The singer sang the note 'la' beautifully.

london.d: A famous city in the country of England
london.e: My family visited London on vacation.

language.d: The way people communicate with words
language.e: The teacher taught us a new language in class.

lead.d: To show the way or go first
lead.e: The teacher will lead the class on a field trip.

lord.d: A very important person, like a king
lord.e: The king is the lord of the castle.

likely.e: It is likely that it will rain tomorrow.

longer.d: For a greater amount of time
longer.e: I can play outside for longer today.

learn.d: To get knowledge or a new skill
learn.e: I'm learning how to bake cookies in my cooking class.

led.d: Past tense of the word 'lead', meaning to show the way or guide.
led.e: The teacher led the students to the library.

lower.d: To move or make something go down to a more bottom or less high position.
lower.e: The sun lowered in the sky as it got later in the day.

lives.d: The way a person or animal exists and grows
lives.e: My grandparents have lived very interesting lives.

looked.d: When you use your eyes to see something
looked.e: I looked out the window to see the birds.

league.d: A group of teams that compete against each other
league.e: The soccer league has many different teams.

legal.d: Something that follows the law and is allowed
legal.e: It is legal for me to ride my bike on the sidewalk.

lose.d: To not be able to find something or for something to disappear
lose.e: I don't want to lose my favorite toy.

loss.d: When something is gone or you don't have it anymore
loss.e: The team felt bad about their loss in the game.

listen.d: To pay attention to sounds or words
listen.e: I listen carefully when my parents give me instructions.

limited.d: When something has a small amount or is not a lot
limited.e: The store had a limited number of toys to buy.

link.e: The chain is the link that holds the swing.

leading.d: being the most important or first in a group
leading.e: The leading runner was ahead of the others.

leaving.e: I am leaving for school in the morning.

letter.d: a written symbol that makes up words
letter.e: My name has five letters in it.

lines.d: straight marks that go across a surface
lines.e: The paper has ruled lines to help me write neatly.

levels.e: The water has reached high levels after the storm.

lack.d: When you don't have enough of something
lack.e: The team has a lack of players, so they can't have a full game.

lady.e: The kind lady at the store helped me find what I needed.

leader.d: A person who guides and directs a group
leader.e: The team captain is the leader of our soccer team.

learning.d: The act of gaining new knowledge or skills
learning.e: I am learning how to play the piano.

laws.d: Rules that everyone has to follow
laws.e: There are laws that say we have to stop at red lights.

lived.d: to have existed or to have been in a certain place
lived.e: My grandparents lived in this house for many years.

loved.d: to have very strong feelings of care and affection for someone or something
loved.e: I love my family more than anything.

luck.d: things that happen by chance, not on purpose
luck.e: I got lucky and won the prize in the raffle.

located.d: to be in a certain place
located.e: The library is located in the center of town.

largest.d: The biggest one of a group
largest.e: The largest apple in the basket is red.

labour.d: The work that people do, especially hard physical work
labour.e: My dad works long hours at his construction labour job.

location.d: The place where someone or something is
location.e: My school is located in the center of the city.

leaders.d: People who guide and direct a group
leaders.e: The team leaders planned the big game strategy.

learned.d: To gain knowledge or skill through study
learned.e: She learned how to ride a bike over the summer.

lol.d: Laughing out loud, which means something is very funny
lol.e: I lol'd when I saw the puppy do a silly dance.

lake.d: A large body of water surrounded by land
lake.e: The family went swimming in the beautiful blue lake.

larger.d: Bigger in size than something else
larger.e: The bigger dog was larger than the smaller one.

lie.d: To say something that is not true
lie.e: She did not want to lie to her parents.

losing.d: When you no longer have something.
losing.e: I don't like losing my favorite toys.

louis.d: A male name meaning famous warrior.
louis.e: My little brother's name is Louis.

le.d: A way to make a word feminine
le.e: The teacher was a kind and helpful lady.

length.d: How long or short something is
length.e: The length of the rope was just right for the project.

los.d: The name of a city in the state of California, USA
los.e: Many famous actors live in Los Angeles, California.

latest.d: The most recent or newest thing.
latest.e: The latest news is that the school is having a party.

leaves.d: The green parts of a plant that grow on the branches.
leaves.e: The trees are losing their leaves in the fall.

library.d: A building with many books that people can borrow.
library.e: We went to the library to find some new books to read.

links.d: Connections or ways to get to other information.
links.e: The website has helpful links to other useful pages.

lucky.d: Having good things happen to you by chance.
lucky.e: I was so lucky to win the prize in the raffle.

labor.d: The work that people do to earn money.
labor.e: My dad works hard at his labor job.

lies.d: When someone says something that isn't true.
lies.e: It's important not to tell lies.

literally.d: Exactly as it sounds, without any changes.
literally.e: The baby literally ate the whole cookie.

liked.d: When someone enjoys or approves of something.
liked.e: My teacher liked the story I wrote.

limit.d: The greatest or smallest amount allowed.
limit.e: There is a time limit to finish the test.

lee.d: the side of something that is sheltered from the wind
lee.e: I sat on the lee side of the tree to get out of the wind.

listed.d: Something that is written down or put on a list.
listed.e: My teacher listed all the books we need to read.

lay.d: To put something down in a flat or horizontal position.
lay.e: The cat likes to lay in the sunshine.

leg.d: One of the body parts you use to walk and stand.
leg.e: The athlete hurt his leg during the game.

legs.d: The body parts you use to walk and stand.
legs.e: The table has four strong legs to hold it up.

letters.d: The symbols or characters used to make up words.
letters.e: The child learned to write the letters of the alphabet.

laughing.d: Laughing is when you make a happy sound and smile because something is funny.
laughing.e: The kids were laughing and playing in the park.

launched.d: Launched means to send something up or out, like a rocket or ship.
launched.e: The astronauts launched into space on a big rocket.

likes.d: Likes are things you enjoy or find pleasing.
likes.e: She likes to read books and play outside.

lovely.d: Lovely means something is very beautiful and nice to look at.
lovely.e: The flower in the garden was lovely and colorful.

lunch.d: Lunch is the meal you eat in the middle of the day.
lunch.e: I had a sandwich for my lunch today.

laugh.d: to make a happy sound with your voice
laugh.e: The funny joke made me laugh.

launch.d: to send something into the air or start something
launch.e: The rocket will launch into space tomorrow.

lawyer.d: a person who knows the law and helps people with legal problems
lawyer.e: The lawyer helped the family with their case.

leads.d: to show the way or be in charge
leads.e: The teacher leads the class to the library.

ladies.e: The ladies in my family love to go shopping.

largely.e: The students are largely excited for the field trip.

linked.e: The two websites are linked so you can move between them.

literature.e: I love to read different kinds of literature.

listening.d: Paying close attention to what someone is saying
listening.e: I am listening carefully to the teacher's instructions.

liberal.d: Willing to accept new ideas and changes
liberal.e: My teacher has a liberal attitude and encourages creativity.

lying.d: Telling something that is not true
lying.e: It's important to never lie to your parents.

latter.d: the second of two things that were mentioned
latter.e: I can choose between chocolate or vanilla, and I think I'll take the latter.

lights.d: Lights are things that give us brightness and help us see in the dark.
lights.e: The lights in my room help me see at night.

languages.e: I'm learning to speak a new language in school

laid.e: The bird laid its eggs in the nest.

legislation.d: a new law or rule that a government makes
legislation.e: The new legislation helps protect the environment.

load.d: a heavy thing that is carried
load.e: The truck carried a big load of supplies.

loves.d: to care about something or someone very much
loves.e: My dog loves to play fetch with me.

loan.d: Borrowing money and promising to pay it back later.
loan.e: The bank gave me a loan to buy a new car.

latin.d: A language that people spoke a long time ago.
latin.e: Many scientific words come from the Latin language.

lane.e: The bike lane is on the left side of the street.

limits.e: There are limits to how much candy you can eat.

loud.e: The barking dog was so loud it woke up the baby.

lands.e: The farm has beautiful green lands for the animals to roam.

lift.d: To move something up or raise it
lift.e: I need to lift the box to put it on the shelf.

les.d: A word used to show there is less of something.
les.e: There were les apples in the basket than yesterday.

letting.e: The teacher was letting the students play outside.

landing.d: the area where you get off of an airplane, ship, or stairs
landing.e: We arrived safely at the airport landing.

lets.d: to allow or permit something to happen
lets.e: The teacher lets the students play outside.
